ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your depression level is within the normal range, with no significant depressive distress detected.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current mental health
Keep up an active lifestyle and social activities
Maintain regular exercise and good sleep habits

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depression. You may occasionally feel low in mood or lack interest, but the impact on daily functioning is limited.
suggestions: Increase engagement in pleasurable activities and social interactions
Maintain regular physical exercise
Pay attention to sleep hygiene and balanced nutrition
Consider consulting a professional if symptoms persist or worsen

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate depression. Depressed mood may be noticeably interfering with your work, social life, or daily activities.
suggestions: Consider seeking help from a counselor or psychiatrist
Try evidence-based psychological treatments such as cognitive-behavioral therapy
Maintain a daily activity schedule and avoid prolonged isolation
Inform a trusted family member or friend about your condition

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depression, which may be significantly impairing your work ability, social functioning, and quality of life.
suggestions: Seek professional psychological or psychiatric treatment as soon as possible
Inform family or friends about your condition for support
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately
Do not suffer alone; professional help can effectively alleviate depression

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score indicates no significant panic or agoraphobia symptoms. You are functioning well in this area.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy coping strategies
Practice regular relaxation and stress management

ranges.label: Mild Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild symptoms of panic or agoraphobia. These symptoms may cause some discomfort but are not significantly impairing.
suggestions: Learn about panic disorder and its symptoms
Practice breathing exercises and grounding techniques
Gradually expose yourself to feared situations with support
Consider consulting a mental health professional if symptoms persist

ranges.label: Moderate Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: You are experiencing moderate panic or agoraphobia symptoms that are likely affecting your daily functioning.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for panic disorder
Practice regular relaxation techniques
Avoid caffeine and other stimulants that may worsen symptoms

ranges.label: Severe Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: Your symptoms are severe and likely causing significant impairment in multiple areas of life.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional treatment promptly
Consider a combination of medication and psychotherapy
Reach out to your support network for help
Do not attempt to face feared situations alone without professional guidance

ranges.label: Very Severe Panic/Agoraphobia
ranges.description: Your symptoms are very severe. Panic attacks and avoidance behavior are likely dominating your daily life. Immediate professional help is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Please contact a mental health professional or visit a hospital immediately
Medication may be necessary to reduce acute symptoms
Ensure you have a safety plan and support person available
Remember that panic disorder is highly treatable with proper care

ranges.label: Minimal Somatic Severity
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom score is minimal. No significant physical symptom burden detected.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Regular exercise and balanced diet support physical well-being
Practice stress management to prevent somatic symptoms

ranges.label: Low Somatic Severity
ranges.description: You are experiencing a low level of somatic symptoms. These may be related to stress or other factors.
suggestions: Monitor your symptoms and note any patterns
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing or yoga
Ensure adequate sleep and regular exercise
If symptoms persist, consult a healthcare provider

ranges.label: Medium Somatic Severity
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom level is moderate. These symptoms may be affecting your daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider consulting a healthcare provider for evaluation
Explore stress management and relaxation strategies
Maintain a symptom diary to identify triggers
Consider whether psychological factors may be contributing

ranges.label: High Somatic Severity
ranges.description: Your somatic symptom level is high. This significant symptom burden warrants professional evaluation.
suggestions: Seek medical evaluation to rule out underlying conditions
Consider consultation with both medical and mental health professionals
Cognitive behavioral therapy can be effective for somatic symptoms
Avoid excessive self-monitoring that may increase symptom focus

ranges.label: Low Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination tendency is low. You have good time management and task completion abilities.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your good action-oriented habits
Share your efficiency strategies with others

ranges.label: Moderate Procrastination
ranges.description: You show some procrastination tendencies that occasionally affect your efficiency and ability to complete tasks on time.
suggestions: Create a daily priority list
Use the 'two-minute rule' — if it takes less than two minutes, do it immediately
Reduce barriers to starting tasks (e.g., prepare your work environment in advance)

ranges.label: High Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination behavior is quite noticeable, frequently causing delays on tasks and deadlines.
suggestions: Consider learning time management techniques
Establish an accountability system with friends or colleagues
Try cognitive-behavioral strategies to combat procrastination thinking
Consider consulting a counselor

ranges.label: Severe Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination problem is very serious, significantly impacting your quality of life and sense of self-efficacy.
suggestions: Recommend seeking professional counseling support
CBT and ACT are effective treatments for procrastination
Start with 'tiny habits' to rebuild your capacity for action
Address the emotional issues behind procrastination (e.g., anxiety, perfectionism)

ranges.label: Good Memory
ranges.description: Your overall memory performance appears to be good, with infrequent memory failures in daily life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current memory strategies
Keep up mentally stimulating activities
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits that support cognitive function

ranges.label: Average Memory
ranges.description: Your memory performance is within the average range. You experience occasional memory lapses that are common in daily life.
suggestions: Use external aids like calendars and reminder apps for important tasks
Establish consistent routines to support prospective memory
Stay mentally active through reading, puzzles, or learning new skills
Ensure adequate sleep, as it is critical for memory consolidation

ranges.label: Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score suggests you experience memory failures more frequently than average. This may affect daily functioning.
suggestions: Implement systematic reminder strategies (phone alarms, sticky notes, to-do lists)
Minimize distractions when trying to remember important information
Ensure adequate sleep, as sleep is critical for memory consolidation
Consider whether stress or multitasking may be contributing to memory lapses

ranges.label: Very Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score indicates frequent memory failures that may significantly impact daily life.
suggestions: Consider consulting a healthcare professional for a thorough memory assessment
Use multiple external memory aids consistently
Simplify routines and reduce cognitive load where possible
Discuss with a professional whether underlying factors may be involved

prospective.label: Good Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You generally remember to carry out planned actions and future intentions.

prospective.label: Average Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You occasionally forget to carry out planned actions, which is within the normal range.

prospective.label: Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You frequently forget to carry out intended actions. Consider using external reminders.

prospective.label: Very Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You very frequently forget planned actions. Systematic use of external aids is strongly recommended.

retrospective.label: Good Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You generally recall past events and previously learned information well.

retrospective.label: Average Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You occasionally have difficulty recalling past events, which is within the normal range.

retrospective.label: Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You frequently have difficulty remembering past events and information.

retrospective.label: Very Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You very frequently have difficulty recalling past events. Consider seeking professional assessment.

ranges.label: Low Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is low. You appear to be coping well with life demands.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current coping strategies
Keep up healthy habits and routines
Practice preventive stress management techniques

ranges.label: Moderate Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is moderate. Consider enhancing your stress management strategies.
suggestions: Identify and address your main sources of stress
Practice relaxation techniques regularly
Maintain work-life balance
Seek social support when needed

ranges.label: High Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is high. This may be affecting your well-being and warrants attention.
suggestions: Consider completing the full PSS-10 for a more detailed assessment
Consult a mental health professional for stress management support
Implement immediate stress-reduction strategies
Review and prioritize your commitments

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your level of perseverative negative thinking is low. You can recover well from negative events.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy thinking habits
Keep up mindfulness practices to sustain psychological resilience

ranges.label: Mild Rumination
ranges.description: You have some degree of repetitive negative thinking and may occasionally get caught in cycles of overthinking.
suggestions: Learn mindfulness meditation to reduce ruminative thinking
Try shifting your attention to positive activities
Practice acceptance and letting-go techniques
Maintain regular physical exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Rumination
ranges.description: Your perseverative negative thinking level is elevated. This may affect your daily functioning and emotional well-being.
suggestions: Consider learning cognitive-behavioral techniques to break the rumination cycle
Consider seeking professional counseling
Practice mindfulness and relaxation techniques
Share your concerns with someone you trust

ranges.label: Severe Rumination
ranges.description: Your perseverative negative thinking level is very high. This may significantly affect your mental health and daily life.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional psychological help
Cognitive-behavioral therapy is very effective in reducing ruminative thinking
Avoid spending time alone overthinking; engage more with others
Monitor for co-occurring depression or anxiety symptoms
